items.NAME: 🏓 Pong
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The classic that started it all — reimagined as a programming challenge. Pong strips game design down to its purest form: a ball, two paddles, and the code you write to control them. Players write real code to drive their paddle's AI, learning fundamentals of game loops, input handling, and physics prediction along the way.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Pong runs as a WebGL build served directly from the site. The interpreter evaluates player-written code each frame to control paddle movement, enabling experimentation with prediction algorithms, reaction timing, and strategic positioning — all through actual programming.

items.NAME: 🎹 Midi
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Music meets programming. Midi turns musical composition into a coding challenge — players write code to generate, sequence, and manipulate MIDI events in real time. Learn about audio programming, event-driven systems, and procedural generation while making actual music.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, the Midi game exposes a programming interface for MIDI I/O, note sequencing, and haptic feedback. Players write programs that compose melodies, build chord progressions, and react to musical input — bridging the gap between creative expression and computational thinking.

items.NAME: 🪖 Tanks
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Program your tank, outsmart the enemy. Tanks is a tactical AI programming game where players write code to control their tank's movement, aiming, and firing systems. Victory goes to the best algorithm, not the fastest reflexes.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Tanks pits player-coded AIs against each other on a battlefield. Players program targeting algorithms, pathfinding routines, and evasion strategies. The game teaches real-world concepts like vector math, state machines, and autonomous decision-making — all through the lens of tank combat.

items.NAME: 🐍 Snake
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The longer you survive, the harder it gets — just like real engineering. Snake turns the iconic arcade loop into a programming playground where players write code to navigate an ever-growing tail through tightening spaces. One wrong move and you're debugging a collision.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Snake lets players program their own pathfinding and decision-making algorithms. Each frame, your code decides the next direction — balancing food collection against self-avoidance. It's a crash course in state management, greedy algorithms, and thinking ahead.

items.NAME: 🐦 Bird
items.TEXT:
The Idea

One tap keeps you airborne — but code keeps you alive. Bird distills the infinite runner into a pure timing challenge where players program their own flap controller. Gravity is constant, pipes are relentless, and your algorithm is the only thing between flight and freefall.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Bird exposes pipe positions and bird velocity to player-written code. Players program when to flap using prediction, threshold logic, or even neural-net-style weightings — learning physics simulation, event timing, and reactive control systems one gap at a time.

items.NAME: ☄️ Asteroids
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Space doesn't care about your feelings — only your vectors. Asteroids throws players into a wraparound arena of drifting debris where survival depends on the thrust, rotation, and firing code you write. No friction, no mercy, no hand-holding.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Asteroids gives players programmatic control over ship thrust, rotation, and projectile firing. Write code that calculates intercept angles, manages momentum in frictionless space, and prioritizes threats — a masterclass in trigonometry, velocity math, and real-time decision-making.

items.NAME: 🧱 Tetris
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Every piece is a decision. Every gap is a bug. Tetris transforms the world's most-played puzzle into a programming arena where players write code to place, rotate, and drop tetrominoes. Clear lines with algorithms, not reflexes.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Tetris exposes the board state, current piece, and next queue to player-written code. Players program placement strategies — from simple column-height heuristics to full lookahead search — learning optimization, heuristic design, and the art of making fast, good-enough decisions under pressure.

items.NAME: ⛃ Checkers
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Simple rules, deep strategy — and your code makes every move. Checkers distills adversarial AI into its purest form: capture, king, and conquer the board with the algorithm you write. No hidden information, no luck — just your logic versus theirs.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Checkers exposes the full board state to player-written code each turn. Players program move evaluation, multi-jump chains, and king promotion strategies — learning minimax search, heuristic scoring, and the foundations of game-tree AI one hop at a time.

items.NAME: ♟️ Chess
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The ultimate test of computational thinking — now yours to program. Chess puts the full 64-square battlefield under your code's control. Write the engine that sees three moves ahead, or thirty. Every grandmaster strategy is just an algorithm waiting to be written.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Chess provides the complete board state, legal move generation, and turn control to player-written code. Players implement everything from simple piece-value heuristics to alpha-beta pruning and positional evaluation — a deep dive into search algorithms, optimization, and strategic abstraction.

items.NAME: 💥 Breakout
items.TEXT:
The Idea

One paddle. One ball. A wall of bricks. Your code decides what survives. Breakout turns brick-busting into a precision programming challenge where players write the paddle AI that tracks, predicts, and clears every last block.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Breakout exposes ball position, velocity, and brick layout to player-written code. Players program paddle movement using trajectory prediction, bounce angle math, and target prioritization — learning physics simulation, interpolation, and real-time control one brick at a time.

items.NAME: 🔴 Connect 4
items.MOTTO: Four in a Row of Code
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Drop a disc, build a strategy. Connect 4 turns the classic vertical grid into a battleground for adversarial AI — players write code that picks the winning column before the opponent does. Simple to learn, deceptively hard to master.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Connect 4 gives player-written code the full board state and asks for one thing: which column to drop in. Players program threat detection, fork setups, and lookahead search — learning game-tree evaluation, pattern recognition, and the art of blocking while attacking.

items.NAME: 🃏 Poker
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Write the bluff. Poker puts incomplete information, probability, and deception into your hands as code. Players program bet sizing, hand evaluation, and opponent modeling to dominate a table of coded competitors — where the best poker face is the best algorithm.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Poker exposes hole cards, community cards, pot odds, and opponent betting history to player-written code. Players program strategies ranging from tight-aggressive fundamentals to advanced range estimation and bluff frequency tuning — learning probability, game theory, and decision-making under uncertainty.

items.NAME: 🂡 Blackjack
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Know when to hold, know when to fold — because your code decided. Blackjack turns the casino classic into a pure probability engine where players program hit, stand, double-down, and split decisions. Beat the house with math, not luck.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Blackjack gives player-written code the current hand value, dealer's visible card, and remaining deck composition. Players implement basic strategy tables, card counting systems, and Kelly criterion bet sizing — learning expected value, conditional probability, and risk management one hand at a time.

items.NAME: 🏎️ Racer
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Victory goes to the fastest algorithm, not the fastest fingers. Racer puts players behind the wheel of a code-driven car — program throttle, braking, and racing lines to lap an AI opponent on procedural tracks.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Racer exposes track geometry, car velocity, tire grip, and opponent position to player-written code. Players program steering controllers, apex calculation, and overtake logic — learning PID control, path optimization, and real-time physics negotiation at top speed.

items.NAME: 💣 Minesweeper
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Every click is a calculated risk — and your code makes the call. Minesweeper challenges players to write a deduction engine that clears the board using neighbor counts, constraint propagation, and probability when logic alone isn't enough.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Minesweeper exposes the revealed board state and mine counts to player-written code. Players program safe-cell identification, flag placement, and probabilistic guessing strategies — learning constraint satisfaction, set intersection logic, and decision-making under uncertainty one square at a time.

items.NAME: 🎱 Pool
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Every shot is a physics equation — your code solves it. Pool brings real billiards physics to a scripting layer where players program cue angle, force, and spin to sink balls with algorithmic precision. No loot boxes, no pay-to-win cues — just math.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Pool exposes ball positions, velocities, table geometry, and friction coefficients to player-written code. Players program shot planning, multi-ball collision prediction, and positional play — learning 2D physics simulation, vector math, and optimization as they clear the table.

items.NAME: 🥊 Fighter
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Read the opponent, execute the combo — all in code. Fighter is a side-scrolling fighting game where players program their fighter's moves, combos, blocking, and spacing. Victory belongs to the sharpest algorithm, not the fastest button masher.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Fighter exposes both fighters' positions, health, meter, and states to player-written code. Players program hit-confirm logic, combo strings, spacing control, and meter management — learning state machines, reaction systems, and frame-perfect decision-making through street-fighter-style combat.

items.NAME: ⛏️ Dwarf
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Every menu click becomes a function call. Dwarf turns fortress management into a programming challenge — dig tunnels, assign labors, brew drinks, and fend off sieges by writing the code that runs your civilization. Your dwarves are only as smart as your scripts.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Dwarf exposes terrain, dwarf states, stockpiles, and threats to player-written code through an Overseer Terminal. Players program excavation blueprints, labor assignment, production queues, and military responses — learning automation, resource management, and systems thinking one z-level at a time.

items.NAME: ⛏ Minecraft
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Survival by script. Minecraft puts a 3D voxel world under your code's control — mine resources, craft tools, build shelters, and fight mobs by writing the programs that keep you alive. Every mouse click becomes a function call.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Minecraft exposes block data, inventory, crafting recipes, and mob positions to player-written code. Players program mining routines, shelter construction, combat logic, and resource management — learning 3D spatial reasoning, state machines, and procedural automation in a hostile voxel world.

items.NAME: 🌋 PopVuj
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Gods don't fight — gods influence. Inspired by the Popol Vuh, PopVuj is a god-sim where you rule from the heavens, shaping a civilization through prophets, smiting, weather, and omens. Your minions have heritable traits — religiousness, independence, strength — and your decrees ripple across generations.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, PopVuj exposes faith, disease, crime, population, and city layout to player-written code. Players program divine intervention strategies — sending prophets, summoning bears, building chapels, and manipulating weather — learning feedback loops, genetic algorithms, and the tension between control and freedom.

items.NAME: ⚔️ Aufstand
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Command the uprising. Aufstand is a tactical rebellion game where players write code to organize resistance, coordinate insurgents, and overthrow a regime. Every order — recruit, sabotage, rally, retreat — is a function call in your revolution's script.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, Aufstand exposes unit positions, morale, supply lines, and enemy patrols to player-written code. Players program recruitment strategies, sabotage timing, and tactical maneuvers — learning resource allocation, risk assessment, and asymmetric warfare through code.

items.NAME: 🏗️ Bootstrap
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The template that starts every CodeGamified game. Bootstrap is the open-source starter kit — a Unity project pre-wired with the CodeGamified engine, TUI system, and simulation clock. Fork it, implement three interfaces, and ship your own educational coding game.

How It Works

Bootstrap provides the full CodeGamified architecture out of the box: a Python-subset compiler, sandboxed VM, retro terminal UI, time-warp controls, and template scripts for compiler extensions, I/O handlers, and match managers. New games inherit the entire programming loop — players write real code from day one.

items.NAME: 👾 Galaga
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Waves of alien formations dive-bomb your ship. Write targeting priorities, dodge patterns, and formation-break logic to survive escalating swarms.

items.NAME: 👑 Royale
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Shrinking arena, multiple opponents, one survivor. Write loot prioritization, zone prediction, and engagement logic to outlast the field.

items.NAME: 💣 Bomber
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Drop bombs on a grid, destroy walls, dodge your own blasts. Write chain-reaction planning and safe-zone pathfinding that accounts for future explosions.

items.NAME: ⛳ Golf
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Calculate shot angle, power, and spin to navigate procedural courses in minimum strokes. Physics meets optimization on every hole.

items.NAME: 🚦 Traffic
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Control traffic signals across an intersection grid. Write timing algorithms that maximize throughput while preventing gridlock and collisions.

items.NAME: 🎖️ Stratego
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Hidden-information war on a 10×10 grid. Write AI that infers enemy ranks from movement patterns, tracks revealed pieces, and decides when to attack vs. scout using probabilistic reasoning.

items.NAME: 🏰 Tower Defense
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Wave-based creep defense. Write targeting policies, tower placement heuristics, upgrade timing, and economic optimization to survive escalating waves on grid maps.

items.NAME: 🧊 Rubik's
items.TEXT:
The Idea

3D cube manipulation with scramble-and-solve progression. Write solve algorithms from brute-force to layer-by-layer to CFOP to Kociemba — chase God's number of 20 moves.

items.NAME: 🚀 Missile Command
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Defend cities from incoming ballistic missiles. Write intercept trajectory prediction, MIRV anticipation, and ammo triage logic across 3 bases protecting 6 cities.

items.NAME: 🌙 Lander
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Land a spacecraft on procedural terrain with thrust and rotation. Write PID controllers, fuel-optimal descent algorithms, and terrain-aware landing routines.

items.NAME: 📦 Sokoban
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Push crates onto targets in a warehouse grid. Write solvers from BFS to A* to deadlock-pruning — the purest search-algorithm teaching game.

items.NAME: 🚢 Battleship
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Hunt-and-sink on dual 10×10 grids. Write targeting algorithms from random to hunt/target to probability density heatmaps that visualize your AI's thinking in real time.

items.NAME: 🪱 Worms
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Turn-based artillery on destructible 2D terrain. Write ballistic calculators with wind compensation, weapon selection logic, and positional strategy on a map that changes with every shot.

items.NAME: 🟡 Pac-Man
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The most recognizable game ever made. Write four distinct ghost AIs — Blinky chases, Pinky ambushes, Inky flanks, Clyde scatters — then write Pac-Man AI that exploits their known behaviors.

items.NAME: 👾 Space Invaders
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The game that created the arcade industry. Write formation movement, speed scaling as numbers thin, shield erosion, and auto-aim targeting logic.

items.NAME: 💥 Bomberman
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Grid-based bomb placement with chain-reaction explosions. Write temporal pathfinding that accounts for future detonations and enemy-trapping strategies.

The Idea

Slide tiles, merge doubles, reach 2048. Write expectimax AI that evaluates all possible moves and random tile spawns to achieve superhuman scores.

items.NAME: 🟩 Wordle
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Guess a 5-letter word in 6 tries. Write an information-theoretic solver that maximizes entropy per guess to crack any word in ≤3.5 guesses on average.

items.NAME: 🃏 Solitaire
items.TEXT:
The Idea

The most played computer game in history. Write a solver that manages tableau stacking, foundation building, and stock cycling under hidden-card uncertainty.

items.NAME: 🐂 Oregon Trail
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Travel 2,000 miles by wagon. Write survival optimizers for pace, rations, hunting, and river crossings — then Monte Carlo simulate 10,000 trails to find the statistically optimal strategy.

items.NAME: 🐹 Lemmings
items.TEXT:
The Idea

100 mindless lemmings march forward. Write a coordinator that assigns jobs — digger, builder, blocker, climber — to route the swarm to the exit with minimum assignments.

items.NAME: 🗡️ Roguelike
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Procedurally generated dungeon crawl with permadeath. Write the dungeon generator (BSP, cellular automata, WFC) AND the player AI. The capstone game — touches every CS concept in the roster.

items.NAME: 🤖 Bot Arena
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Students write bots. Bots fight each other. No manual play — the code IS the player. Scan, move, shoot, shield. Battle replays render as visual combat. The game that makes CodeGamified social.

items.NAME: 🛸 Space Trader
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Fly between star systems trading commodities. Write route optimizers, dynamic pricing models, and risk management for a procedural star map with supply/demand economics.

items.NAME: 🍄 Platformer
items.TEXT:
The Idea

2D side-scroller. Write the character controller (gravity, collision, coyote time), enemy AI (patrol, chase, return), and procedural level generator. The game every student imagines first.

items.NAME: 🏛️ Civ-Lite
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Turn-based 4X on a hex grid. Write the AI that founds cities, researches tech, builds armies, and negotiates diplomacy. The most complex system a student can build.

items.NAME: 🌀 Maze
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Write both sides: generate labyrinths (DFS, Kruskal's, Prim's) then solve them (wall-follower, BFS, A*, fog-of-war). The only game where creation and solution are algorithmic duals.

items.NAME: 💻 Hacker
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Network intrusion sim. Traverse a graph of nodes, breach firewalls, exfiltrate data, avoid trace. Write scripts against a system — the code-to-game metaphor collapses entirely. The most on-brand game possible.

items.NAME: ⚽ Soccer
items.TEXT:
The Idea

2D top-down soccer. Write the team brain — formations, passing lanes, marking assignments, set pieces. The only game where you program 11 cooperative agents simultaneously.

items.NAME: 🎵 Rhythm
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Notes fall down lanes — hit the key on beat. Write auto-players, MIDI-to-chart parsers, difficulty scalers, and beat map generators. Extends the existing Midi infrastructure into a game.

items.NAME: 🥷 Stealth
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Top-down infiltration through guard patrols and camera sweeps. Write temporal pathfinding that threads between vision cones, waits in shadows, and chains distractions to open corridors.

items.NAME: 📈 Idle
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Click to earn, buy producers, prestige to reset with multipliers. Write ROI calculators, prestige timing optimizers, and production pipeline planners that manage exponential growth systems.

items.NAME: 🏕️ Survival
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Procedural island, day/night cycles, escalating threats. Write daily planners that optimize resource gathering during daylight and ensure survival through the night. Adds stakes to the sandbox.

items.NAME: 🏍️ Tron
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Leave neon trails, turn 90°, don't crash. Two commands. Write flood-fill evaluation, Voronoi partitioning, and opponent modeling. Simplest API, deepest strategy. Natural bot-vs-bot entry.

items.NAME: ⚔️ RPG Combat
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Turn-based party combat. 4 heroes vs. enemy formations. Write damage formula exploitation, elemental matchup AI, status effect strategy, and party composition optimizers across multi-encounter dungeons.

items.NAME: 🗃️ Deckbuilder
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Start weak, win cards, build synergies, survive the spire. Write combat AI that reads enemy intent and a deck curation strategy that optimizes card synergy across an entire run.

items.NAME: 🌾 Farm
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Seasonal farming on a grid. Plant, water, harvest, sell. Write crop rotation planners, weather adaptation logic, market timing strategies, and multi-season profit optimizers.

items.NAME: 🔑 Escape Room
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Single-screen room, click objects, combine items, decode clues. Write solvers that model the room as a dependency DAG and topological-sort the solution path through red herrings.

items.NAME: 🏥 Management
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Run a hospital, restaurant, or theme park. Write customer routing, staff assignment, queue optimization, layout planning, and dynamic pricing to keep everyone happy and solvent.

items.NAME: ✈️ Dogfight
items.TEXT:
The Idea

1v1 aerial combat in a 2D wrap-around arena. Write pursuit curves, lead targeting, evasion maneuvers, and energy management. The only combat game with a continuous flight model.

items.NAME: 🏴‍☠️ SeaRäuber
items.TEXT:
Logic Meets Fate

In SeaRäuber, logical mastery is only the first chapter of the story. While your code builds ships, directs fleets, and governs an empire, fate's hand — revealed through tarot — challenges your carefully laid plans. Will prophecy bring prosperity or disaster? Each card draw brings new challenges, forcing you to adapt, overcome, or embrace the chaos. In this world, strategy bends to fate, and only those who balance logic and fortune will leave a lasting legacy.

items.NAME: ☄ BitNaughts
items.TEXT:
The Idea

BitNaughts is a free-to-play educational programming video game: code gamified. Construct your object-oriented spaceship, learn programming to code your systems, and save the Earth from Martian invasion. Born from Iterate's interpreter and refined across four consecutive Microsoft Hackathons, BitNaughts turns real coding into real gameplay.

How It Works

Built in Unity 6.0 with C#, the project is organized as a set of Git submodules: a Unity project (bitnaughts.unity), a custom C# interpreter (bitnaughts.interpreter) that steps through each OP Code of abstracted languages, a WebGL front-end (bitnaughts.github.io) for browser-based play, a serverless Azure back-end (bitnaughts.mainframe) for persistence, and separate repos for assets, UI/UX, and components. Players write actual code to control their ship's systems (navigation, weapons, shields) with the interpreter evaluating their programs in real time against the game's physics and combat engine.

The Grind

Four Microsoft Hackathon pitches ('20, '21, '22, '23) shaped the vision through iterative live-demo feedback, each year pushing the architecture further: from a monolithic prototype to a fully modular, open-source game engine. The submodule architecture means contributors can clone, modify, and submit changes to any layer independently. The core challenge remains the same one Iterate cracked: making programming genuinely fun, not just gamified busywork.

items.NAME: 🔄 Iterate
items.TEXT:
The Idea

Iterate is a mobile code editor that uses Java/Arduino syntax but lets you build programs through guided tap-based selections instead of raw typing. It sits between block-based tools like Scratch and writing real code. Built for UC Merced's CITRIS Mobile App Challenge.

How It Works

Built in Unity with C#, the app runs on a 1,300+ line CodeManager that acts as both editor and interpreter. Users tap a line to enter edit mode, then construct code through cascading option menus: Create Variable → Integer/Double/Boolean/String → name it → assign a value. Flow control (if/else, while, for loops), Arduino hardware calls (pinMode, digitalWrite, analogRead, analogWrite, Serial.begin/print/println, servo.attach/write, delay, map), and variable scoping are all handled through the same tap-driven UI. A ColorCoder class provides real-time syntax highlighting (keywords in blue, objects in teal, strings in amber, comments in green) by injecting Unity rich text color tags on every frame. Pre-built lesson templates (Windmill, Servos, Stopwatch) load from Resources as starting scaffolds.

The Grind

The editor tracks variable scope by scanning every line above the cursor for declarations, dynamically populating only valid options at each step; you cannot write a syntax error. The whole thing compiles to WebGL for in-browser play on any device. $5,000 Grand Prize at the 2017 CITRIS Mobile App Challenge.
